findings.symptom: Decoded BTR records and BTR instances are double-cast into structural field records.
findings.root_cause: BoundaryTransitionRecord fields are not decoded through a runtime constructor or boundary codec.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce BoundaryTransitionRecord and a boundary codec that returns validated fields before domain construction.

proposed_nouns.name: BoundaryTransitionRecord
proposed_nouns.constructs: BoundaryTransitionRecord constructor from validated BoundaryTransitionFields supplied by a boundary codec or domain factory.
proposed_nouns.consumes: BTR verification, replay, and provenance use-cases consume it instead of structural field bags.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: A BTR has the required version, input hash, output hash, initial-state bytes, provenance, timestamp, and authentication tag.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/provenance/BTR.ts decoded-object-to-BTRFields double cast and BTR instance-to-record validation cast.

findings.symptom: Provenance payload entries are double-cast between BTR payload shape and PatchEntry.
findings.root_cause: BoundaryTransitionProvenance and BTR wire patch-entry shapes are not separated.
findings.recommended_fix: Model domain PatchEntry and BoundaryTransitionProvenance separately from BtrWirePatchEntry.

proposed_nouns.name: BoundaryTransitionProvenance
proposed_nouns.constructs: A provenance domain factory constructs it from already-decoded PatchEntry values.
proposed_nouns.consumes: BoundaryTransitionRecord creation and replay consume it as the ordered provenance sequence.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Every provenance entry is a replayable domain PatchEntry with a patch and commit SHA, not a wire record.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/provenance/btrOperations.ts payload.toJSON() and btr.P as unknown as PatchEntry[] bridges.

findings.symptom: Generic snapshot clone returns arbitrary T by cast.
findings.root_cause: Immutable snapshot construction is procedural and lacks a named snapshot value or builder.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce SnapshotMaterializer or ImmutableSnapshot value semantics before removing casts.

findings.symptom: BTR imports CodecPort/defaultCodec and owns serialize/deserialize behavior in domain code.
findings.root_cause: BTR wire encoding is not owned by an adapter or codec boundary.
findings.recommended_fix: Move BTR encoding and decoding to BoundaryTransitionRecordCodecPort or adapter-owned codec implementation.

proposed_nouns.name: BoundaryTransitionRecordCodecPort
proposed_nouns.constructs: Adapter or codec implementations construct decoded BoundaryTransitionRecord values and canonical BTR byte products.
proposed_nouns.consumes: Application/provenance use-cases depend on the port instead of importing defaultCodec in domain code.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Wire encode/decode and canonical byte production happen at the boundary, not inside the domain BTR model.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/provenance/BTR.ts CodecPort/defaultCodec import, serialize(), and deserialize() boundary leakage.

findings.symptom: Domain-side HMAC computation uses CodecPort/defaultCodec to encode signing data.
findings.root_cause: Canonical BTR signing bytes are not modeled as a boundary product.
findings.recommended_fix: Have a boundary codec produce BtrSigningBytes; HMAC code signs only bytes.

findings.symptom: PropertyIndexReader decodes shard bytes and parses raw decoded values inside domain service code.
findings.root_cause: DecodedPropertyShard is not produced at an adapter/codec boundary.
findings.recommended_fix: Move property shard decode into a boundary codec or IndexStore path that returns a named shard value.

findings.symptom: PatchEntryJSON is a Record<string, ...> instead of a real model.
findings.root_cause: BTR payload and wire patch-entry concepts are unnamed.
findings.recommended_fix: Split domain PatchEntry/BoundaryTransitionProvenance from transport-only BtrWirePatchEntry.

findings.symptom: computeHmac accepts inline primitive fields as the signing envelope.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce BtrSigningEnvelope as a named value and produce BtrSigningBytes at the boundary.

proposed_nouns.name: BtrSigningEnvelope
proposed_nouns.constructs: BoundaryTransitionRecord creates it from validated record fields excluding the authentication tag.
proposed_nouns.consumes: BoundaryTransitionRecordCodecPort consumes it to produce canonical signing bytes.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: The signed semantic fields are explicit, complete, ordered by concept, and do not include kappa.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/provenance/btrOperations.ts inline fields object passed to computeHmac.

findings.symptom: Decoded property shard state is represented as nested Record bags.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce a named decoded shard model with explicit node-property entries.

proposed_nouns.name: PropertyShard
proposed_nouns.constructs: A property shard decoder or index store constructs it from encoded shard bytes or existing shard artifacts.
proposed_nouns.consumes: PropertyIndexReader consumes it for node property lookup instead of nested record bags.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Decoded shard data contains explicit node ids and property entries in the property-index shard format.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/index/PropertyIndexReader.ts Record<string, Record<string, unknown>> decoded shard model.

findings.symptom: HMAC signs an anonymous semantic object through a codec-selected encoding path.
findings.root_cause: Canonical BTR signing bytes are not modeled as a named boundary product.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce BtrSigningBytes produced by a boundary codec/adapter; crypto signs only those bytes.

proposed_nouns.name: BtrSigningBytes
proposed_nouns.constructs: BoundaryTransitionRecordCodecPort adapter/implementation constructs this domain value from BtrSigningEnvelope using the canonical BTR signing encoder.
proposed_nouns.consumes: The application HMAC flow consumes BtrSigningBytes through CryptoPort.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: The HMAC input is canonical bytes for the BTR signing envelope, not an arbitrary semantic object.

findings.symptom: Verification recomputes expected HMAC from object fields instead of named signing bytes.
findings.root_cause: Signing envelope and canonical byte production are implicit.

findings.symptom: An object with readBlob only is cast to IndexStoragePort.
findings.root_cause: Property shard blob reading is not modeled as its own capability.
findings.recommended_fix: Design PropertyShardReaderPort or equivalent and wire PropertyIndexReader to the narrow capability.

proposed_nouns.name: PropertyShardReaderPort
proposed_nouns.constructs: Adapter implementations construct the capability from blob storage, index storage, or CAS-backed shard storage.
proposed_nouns.consumes: PropertyIndexReader consumes the narrow port for property shard reads.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: The reader depends only on property-shard read capability and not on write/tree/ref index storage operations.

findings.symptom: A readBlob surface is cast to IndexStoragePort for property reader construction.
findings.root_cause: Materialized view load path lacks an honest property-shard reader capability.
findings.recommended_fix: Split the read-only property shard dependency from the full index storage port.

findings.symptom: Deep clone/freeze helpers promise to preserve arbitrary T through Object.create and descriptor copying.
findings.root_cause: Immutable snapshots are implemented as generic object mechanics instead of a named snapshot model.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce ImmutableSnapshot/SnapshotMaterializer and stop returning arbitrary T unless a constructor or snapshot protocol proves it.

proposed_nouns.name: ImmutableSnapshot
proposed_nouns.constructs: SnapshotBuilder constructs it from an explicit supported snapshot source such as WarpState.
proposed_nouns.consumes: Public read-side materialization APIs consume and return it as a read-only snapshot value.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: The value is detached from mutable runtime state and exposes only snapshot-safe read behavior.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/ImmutableSnapshot.ts cloneImmutableObject<T>() arbitrary T preservation cast.

proposed_nouns.name: SnapshotBuilder
proposed_nouns.constructs: Materialization code constructs it with explicit snapshot protocols for supported domain objects.
proposed_nouns.consumes: ImmutableSnapshot construction consumes it as the only supported snapshot materialization path.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Snapshot creation is limited to known domain shapes or explicit snapshot protocols, not arbitrary objects.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/ImmutableSnapshot.ts Object.create descriptor-copy generic clone path.

findings.symptom: checkpointPolicy is optional and only validated when supplied.
findings.root_cause: MaterializationSnapshotPolicy default-on behavior is not modeled.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce MaterializationSnapshotPolicy with default-on behavior and explicit opt-out.

proposed_nouns.name: MaterializationSnapshotPolicy
proposed_nouns.constructs: Runtime/open option normalization constructs it with default-on behavior or explicit opt-out.
proposed_nouns.consumes: Runtime materialization and checkpoint controllers consume it before deciding whether to write a snapshot.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Snapshot creation behavior is explicit and default-on instead of absent when checkpointPolicy is omitted.

findings.symptom: Auto-checkpointing returns immediately when checkpoint policy is absent.
findings.root_cause: Snapshot creation is treated as optional configuration rather than default graph behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Default to snapshot-on policy with retention and opt-out.

proposed_nouns.name: SnapshotRetentionPolicy
proposed_nouns.constructs: Runtime/open option normalization constructs it from defaults or explicit retention options.
proposed_nouns.consumes: Snapshot/checkpoint creation and cleanup paths consume it after writing materialization snapshots.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Default-on snapshotting has bounded retention semantics and does not imply unbounded repository growth.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/RuntimeHost.ts checkpoint absence causing no snapshot write and no retention decision.

findings.symptom: seek creates a new worldline source but does not create a materialization snapshot by default.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce SeekSnapshotPolicy so seek snapshots by default according to retention thresholds.

proposed_nouns.name: SeekSnapshotPolicy
proposed_nouns.constructs: Worldline or runtime seek option normalization constructs it from default materialization snapshot policy.
proposed_nouns.consumes: Worldline.seek and seek-cache/materialization paths consume it when deciding whether to snapshot a sought coordinate.
proposed_nouns.proves_invariant: Seek snapshot behavior is explicit, default-on, and governed by retention rather than hidden behind absent options.
proposed_nouns.eliminates: src/domain/services/Worldline.ts seek returning a new source without a default snapshot decision.

findings.symptom: No concrete lifecycle-soup blocker was classified during PULL.
findings.root_cause: This family remains part of the atlas taxonomy for subsequent scans.
findings.recommended_fix: Add concrete findings when lifecycle objects with optional-field state machines are encountered.

findings.symptom: No concrete junk-drawer blocker was classified during PULL.
findings.root_cause: This family remains part of the atlas taxonomy for subsequent scans.
findings.recommended_fix: Add concrete findings when modules mix unrelated codec, persistence, domain, crypto, and transport concerns.
